policy_note: Every source file is held to 90% line coverage. Debt entries cleared 2026-05-17, then a single entry re-added 2026-05-24 post Option→Result sibling-trait migration: `markov_residual/engine.rs` dropped to 89% because the new `.ok_or_else(|| BackendFailure)` paths fire only when internal helpers (rs_decode_step_walk, recompute_kv, executor.run_*_layer) return None. Triggering those needs the mock EngineBackend infrastructure that was deliberately scoped out (the 2026-05-24 coverage clearance documented that decision); the entry exists to track the debt rather than gold-plate. If a file regresses further the gate fails; raise it back up by adding tests, not by lowering the floor. 2026-06-06: standard.rs (89.5%) and generation.rs (87.3%) regressed when the MoE-aware KV-engine work landed — the new resident-weights dispatch entry points (`prefill_resident`/`decode_step_resident` and `generate_with_engine_resident`, the `--moe-shards`/`LARQL_Q4K_DIRECT_ATTN` paths) plus the `generate_with_engine_from_hidden` break arms were uncovered. RETIRED 2026-06-06: both baselines removed after adding CPU tests that drive those paths directly — the resident path needs no remote-shard/mock-EngineBackend infra (with `LARQL_Q4K_DIRECT_ATTN` unset the CPU backend ignores the threaded index and runs the f32 path), so a StandardEngine over `make_test_q4k_weights` + `make_test_q4k_vindex`, plus an MM/resident-capable StubEngine for the failure/break arms, covers them. standard.rs 89.5→95.2%, generation.rs 87.3→92.1%, both back over the 90% floor. The `markov_residual/engine.rs` entry below is pre-existing and unrelated (still tracks its own scoped-out debt).
